reviewed.by: Product/feature reel for Study Plans + the Advent '25 Questions Before Christmas' challenge (no new doctrinal argument; Incarnation framed per John 1:14). apologia-orthodoxy gate CLEAN — Incarnation framing Nicene (anti-adoptionist 'the Word became flesh', not 'became God'); denominational neutrality held ('family or church' = community invite, topic list = creedal-essentials + rival-worldview apologetics); 0 clarifier candidates. Argument/product-accuracy self-checked vs study-plans.html: 10 guided plans + 2 seasonal challenges (Advent 25 / Easter 40), Beginner–Advanced, 14–30 day range, Read/Ask/Debate/Reflect activity types — all accurate, no overstatement. 'Lent' label dropped from scene to match voiceover + keep season-neutral.

voiceover: Christmas is coming — and this year you can walk toward it one question at a time. The Advent Challenge: twenty-five questions before Christmas, one a day, from the ancient prophecies to the wonder of 'the Word became flesh.' Walk it on your own, or with your whole family or church. And it's just one way in. Apologia Daily has ten guided study plans — a path for every question. These aren't just reading lists. Every day gives you something to do: read the evidence, ask the AI tutor, step into the Debate Arena, then reflect and make it your own. Pick your depth — a fourteen-day crash course, or a thirty-day deep dive — from your very first questions to advanced debate prep. Your progress saves as you go, and you can study solo or with a group. The Resurrection. God's existence. The deity of Christ. Answering Islam. Bible reliability. Worldviews. Whatever the question, there's a path through it. Advent before Christmas. Forty days before Easter. Start free today at apologia daily dot com.
source: study-plans.html — 10 guided Study Plans + 2 seasonal challenges (Advent 25 / Easter 40). Product/feature reel; no new doctrinal argument (Incarnation framing per John 1:14).

scenes.kicker: AND IT’S JUST ONE WAY IN

lines.t: Study solo, or with a group.
